text: One hour holds. Not helping customers with their issues. This is the T-Mobile way.

This review is for Rej (Reg?). Thank you for your amazing customer service. I had trouble with data connection and issues with my phone, on top of that, my old phone was shutting down and I was unable to use it. Rej provided outstanding customer service and was really kind, empathetic, and professional. Thank you for all your help and assistance!💫🌸

I was told that my account was canceled multiple times and it wasn't. They continue to demand payment and refuse to work with me. It takes a lot of time to get a hold of a customer service rep. When I do they contradict each other and are pretty much completely unhelpful. A total nightmare to deal with. Just go with a different provider.

I just got mobile service and continue to drop calls. My internet box never worked. My phone was hacked first month and I had 4 gran taken out of my bank acct. Viruses, they nickel and dine u, only want to help u if u buying something.

TMobile Customer Service Stinks.  I was on hold over hour and a half which is totally unacceptable.  I am going to consider changing my service because customer service is as much important.  They treat you as though once your a customer its ok to slack in other areas but so not the case.

I have been with Sprint for 20+ years now T-Mobile. I was shopping to see if I should switch to ATT with my wife's legacy plan or have her move with me on my Sprint legacy plan. I called the T-Mobile customer services line as I was shopping for rates they told me that I would get a 20% discount if I went to a different plan and I could add my wife. They send me a link I fill out the information. T-Mobile sent me an email to call they confirmed it's 15%. As I was ready to switch the contract, I double-checked I called the number again 877-384-0296, and yet again they said yes it's 15% and I am eligible for it. After they switched me to a more expensive plan there was no 15% discount, I called them and they said they apologized but I am not eligible for the 15% discount. I have spent hours on the phone they refused to pull the recording and listen to it to honor what they told me twice. I am currently looking to leave T-Mobile as it has been a nightmare for me since they bought Sprint. I highly recommend you do not do business with companies that do not honor their words especially when you have been with them for 20 years. I am looking to exit my contract with them and sign up with a different carrier. Haresh P.

T-MOBILE  isn't a the phone company you will want to us. While we used them we had nothing but issues and costumer service couldn't ever fix the problem. Once we canceled with them we found out they had been charging us for an additional line the whole time. It's now been 3 month after closing our account and they again took money from our account. We spoke to two different managers and one says we still owed money (which we dont) and the other says "I see everything is paid in full and I'm not sure why we took another payment. We can reimburse you, however, it's going to take about 60 days." 

We will never use T-MOBILE  again!

THEY ARE NOTHING BUT LIARS! Their advertising is false, they DETEST THE MILITARY, they do not stand by their products, & their customer service, especially Shane, will lie from the first word out of their mouths! HORRIBLE HORRIBLE BUSINESS!!! Oh & the read from a scripts & just keep at it. Liars, all of them!

I've been a customer for almsot 20 years. Recently, I added my wife onto our plan. 

1) they kept trying to sell me on their "new plan" that would cost us $40 more each month. Then when I kept pushing to get her into my plan that Iw as grandfathered into, they were reluctant. 

2) She was eligible for a brand new phone since she was porting-in her number from a different carrier. They gave us options on which one to pick and that each of them were eligible for the promotion. Unfortuantely that wasn't true either- apparently in the fine fine print of a 30page document, this one color and MB sizing wasn't eligible. So now I'm being charged the full $850 for the phone when they said they would cover $720 of it. 

3) After we had signed, the sales person was supposed to call in to help with the port-in but never called. I called customer service to help with the port in and it took 30 minutes of holding and getting moved around until someone could help us.

4) They put the autopay 'bonus' into the sales promotion. So wahtever you see for cost, add $5 more to it (per line mind you).

5) I call every month now to negotiate the promotion they screwed us out of, spend about 1 hour - 2 hours of every month on hold to get this promotion added on. They keep doing a 1 time fee reversal but that's it. so I have to keep calling to get this on. And they try to get you on autopay so you wouldn't notice. Hence they are really charging $40 more than they should be. 

All in all, my experience was fine with them at the beginning but lately, its been atrocious and customer service is nice enough (hence why i gave it 2 stars instead of 1) but the whole system is quite murky to ensure you pay more.

Customer service is in a different country than where I'm at, U.S. They don't know the US and they couldn't find my address in Vermont. And it took them 40 minutes on hold to find the nearest T-MOBILE store in Burlington (after I gave them the location). I found it on Google maps in 3 minutes but stayed on hold until they found it. So bad.

T-Mobile is a fraud and criminals. I traded in my new phone and they didn’t meet the deal they promised. They cancelled the contract and I had to call every month to get a credit. They stole my phone and then wouldn’t give me my monthly credit. I ended up having to pay for the phone outright and move to Verizon. Do not ever use Tmobile services!

First Time Dealing With Customer Service Where The Employees Seemed To Not Have A Solution To Anything Or They Was Just Guessing At It But It Started From Me Trying To File A Claim On My Phone Because I Couldn’t Call My Girlfriend Unless We Was Connected To The Same Wi-Fi And They Ran Test And It Was Something To Do With The Phone So I Went Through With The Claim People And The Man Told Me All I Had To Do Was Take The Phone In Store He Already Sent My Info And They Would Switch It Out Which Wasn’t The Case At All And On Top Of That I Had Sent My Black Iphone Back Still New Damn Near No Scratches Nothing Never Drop Water Damage Nothing I Get To The Store To Pick Up My New Phone And It’s White Which They Didn’t Tell Me Before Hand So The Woman Ask If I Wnated To Wait I Could Return This One And I’m Literally Done With Them At This Point Before Hand To Even Get The Clain The Customer Service Calls Getting Nothing But Disappointment Because The First Rep Told Me They Noted Everything Down So If The Steps Didn’t Work If I Had To Call Back It Would Already Show We Did All The Trouble Shooting And Just To Send Me To The Claims Department Just To Find Out Nothing Was Noted So I’m Trying To Explain To The Man I Just Want To Take Out The Claim I’ve Already Did All The Steps Which I Get Hung Up On After Him Saying He Was Going To Transfer For The Line And To Make A Long Story Short It Seemed That They Hired The First People They Step Out Side And Seen Like It Was Horrible I’ve Never Felt Disrespected By A Company As Much As I Did By Them It Was Like No Concern About My Issue At All But I Ended Up Suing Them And Not Even Getting What Was Agreed By Choice Because It Wasn’t More So About The Money With Me But The Lack Of Service That Many People Pay For That Wasn't Met if Your Going To Run A Billion Dollar Company At least Pay For Some Employees That’s Going To Keep A Business Looking Good Not Ones That’s Going To Get You A Lawsuit

Tmobile did not have my correct email address om file and when I had fraud detected from my bank account my automatic payments were no longer going thru to Tmobile, however they sent the alerts only to a bad email address and waited 3 months before my service was shut ff which alerted me to the need to update my auto payment. I have since paid over $500 per month due to all of the reconnect fees that my bill is consistently over $1000 a month regardless if I contact them prior to the phones being shut off and then I am promised that no fees will be assessed buy then the next person I talk to tells me the reason it is so high is due to the fees and then they promise me to not charge 
E but do anyways. And then proceed to shut my phone service off again 2 weeks after I've just paid them over $500!!! 

In addition I had insurance on each line because 2 of them are for children.  My 10 year old first lost his phone and thr claim was denied for fraud and I could not speak to anyone with the knowledge of how or why my claim was fraud. I then got him another phone I paid for out of pocket and then he broke the screen amd again insurance said it was fraud and would not replace it. I requested tmobike to cancel my insurance on every line and to reimburse me of each month I have paid for insurance ewhen I have been denied my coverage.
And now my phone is shut off every two weeks without fail regardless that I am paying about $500 every 2 weeks for 4 phone lines!! 

DO NOT GO THROUGH TMOBILE!! THEY ARE LIARS AND WILL NOT FOLLOW THROUGH WITH PROGRAMS SUCJ AS INSURANCE THAT HOU PAY MONTHLY FOR BUT WILL NOT BE ABLE TO UTILIZE NOR EVEN GET AN EXPLANATION AS TO WHY IT IS SUPPOSEDLY FRAUD! 
WILL NEVER RECCOMMENE TMOBILE THEY ARE A SCAM AND A HORRIBLE COMPANY WHO WILL CONTINUOISLY ROB PEOPLE BLIND AND SMILE WHILE THEY DO IT!!

My bill is &35 to &50 more than I was told.
Tried to pay a friend's bill but they wanted so much security that Home Land Security was impressed. My "unlimited" everything is sp slow at times I could use a string and two soup cans to download. Customer service, although friendly, is totally useless.
Wondering if t mobile can spell "Boost",  "Metro PCS" or "Straightslk".
Gone from a 1 -10, 10 rating of wow to a 4 of what is wrong with you morons.
The device is locked and cripples the  dual sim feature especially when traveling. The customer support advised to get another phone for travel. So sad!

The customer service aspect of t-mobile is not any better than it was 5 years ago. 
For those considering a switch to T-Mobile internet, you may want to give it caredul thought. 
The service may be shutting down orpossibly throttled late in the evening. However, I can not identify if it is the internwt service, a third party service platform ir a particular program.
T-Mobile offers the "perk" of Paramount+ Showtime. The perk part of it is only the convenience of billing through T-Mobile. I am thinking the benefit runs to T- Mobile rather than the customer.
Paramount promises local channels. For CBS, I can access TX, CA, GA,BOS, PA, MO, FL and many other stations but not CBS in Tucson. I spent hours on the phone with Paramount+ but one is not allowed to speaj to tech support. It goes on from their. It does not seem that Paramount+ is interested in fulfilling its obligations.

I have been with sprint / t mobile for 17 years never ever had a problem with sprint but tmobile I was late on my bill one time in 17 years and they want to charge 160 for a reconnect fee on top of my 190 bill I am beyond mad I might go to Verizon

Same thing bait and switch. Said credit for phone then never mentioned I would have to pay for the phone for two. My bad for not reading the fine print but the store made it sound like the phone was paid off and the contract was for two years.

you cant reach them if you are outside of us.

İf you think you will never need to ask something or never face a problem, its good. Othervise CHOOSE AT T ATLEAST YOU CAN SPEAK WITH SOMEONE OMG

Absolute horror story beginning to end. Wrong trade in phone written on the order, had to return brand new phone and completely start over after hours in the store and three days waiting for the phone. Then SIM card they installed in the old phone wasn’t  activated, was told it was xfinity’s fault and wasted hours trying to get it resolved only to find out it was TMobile all along. Another week of no service. Then phone couldn’t be activated after 4.5 hours, was told it was their connection but it didn’t work at our home either. Finally got issue resolved after multiple days, one hour trips each way and several hours in the store, calls, texts, etc. Then received notification our checking account was debited, wanted to use a credit card from the beginning, wasn’t told it would be an additional $20 per month. Was told we received a credit for trade in phone against out of pocket costs, which never happened. Several calls to customer service later our service is more expensive than we were told, there’s an additional charge for a phone and the credit doesn’t offset it, and no one seems able to understand the simple math that $120 credit against $120 charges equals $0. What a mistake.

T Mobile SUCKS. Worst thing I ever did switching. Dropped calls when in 5 bar service area, texts that never get delivered or people tell me they sent a text and I never get them. It's not random either, poor service all the time.

About a year ago I decided to switch  from Verizon after 38 years to T Mobile since they were offering big deals to switch. WHAT A MISTAKE!!!!!!
Their customer service is awful, their deals are not what they promise and they cannot fix their issues. 
After 6 days I went back to Verizon. This experience Cost me about $100 and a lot of headaches!!!!! 
Think twice before you change your service!!!!

You’re not compatible in my area stop your lies t mobile. Once I pay my last phone off I’m switching my service.

T mobile  sucks balls after  switching  from Verizon  to this CS All I get is stories switching through t. Mobile supposed to give me $800 for a switch. It over. Then mess up my credit card. I will personally sur  those  scumbags Every operator you get on he phone is not on my recoricken overseas hindus, the sky disgusting

Switching tmobile has been nothing but a nightmare,I came from spectrum mobile on the Verizon network , never had any issues using data in my home ,  my data speed on the 4g lte network was around 140 megabit per second download but verizon has no 5g network I'm my area so I came to tmobile there 5g speed is abouy 50 megabits Now I have no data connection in my home I have towers everywhere around me there is no excuse. 1 week in to the plan data just disappears in and out constantly,   I got mixed reasons chat says tower upgrade then chat again says no tower upgrade and phone support says tower upgrade ,  don't they let customers know their services will be interrupted? 
Trade your device with T-MOBILE is far from trade ins.  When you take your car for a trade in the trade value gets deducted from the new car sale .. not as a credit..smh but here it's we buy your device and just give you credit on your account so you can buy more tmobile products with your credit 
It's not a trade in 
Now I'm looking to go back to my old carrier

In November 2021, I traded-in 3 phones (family plan) for a total trade-in value of $2400 ($800 each) at T-mobile. T-mobile promised to return the $2400 in the form of a one time credit and further credits spread over 30 months. However, I have had to call in dozens of time spending close to 50 hours on the phone with them to get the credits. Each time, I'm promised a refund only to see a small partial credit. Each time, I argue with the company to honor the contract, I'm assured a refund of the payments due. 

After I filed a on the phone  complaint with the FCC, t-mobile now tells me they will honor the trade in credit but start the 30 month cycle 13 months after the original contract date. They are not willing to give in writing Tbey abrogated the contract.

Given this is affecting thousands of customers, it is possible T-mobile swindles customers of a couple of billion $ every year.

If we get more than 40 customers willing to go to court, we could file a class action lawsuit against T-mobile?

If this service had 0 stars I would give zero stars to T Mobile Sales and Billing. I am not sure that the promises made by Telesales are real. So far, there is a discrepancy between the Tele-sales offer and the billing. I agreed to transfer five lines from ATT. I have the pdf file with the chat with Tmobile sales. The deal was for $176 per month including taxes and fees after some 20% insider code promo—also, four "free" iPhones 14s with no trade-in. I signed a contract for the New four phones 14s for $129 per month but  I was told the promo for moving from att would give me an equal credit of $129 every month for 24 months. When I called the billing they told me my monthly will be $280 per month and only two phones are "free" the other two  I have to pay for. If I complete the Port  from ATT to T mobile I would pay $80 more per month and have to pay for the iPhone 14s. The customer service is hard to understand and there is a lot of background noise. Some call centers overseas. The last person helping finally told me verbally that indeed I was going to get what I was promised but it could not be put in writing. (leap of faith?) I am tempted to return the unopened four iPhone 14s to T mobile and keep ATT, I have only transferred one line with a phone I own.  I have not ported the other four lines because I suspect it will be a billing nightmare. I have spent close to 5 hours dealing with billing so they can not guarantee my bills to reflect what TElesales sold me on. Legally, they made me sign an installment purchase for the phones but all the promotions are verbal statements or in the chat.  No one seems to care about the chat transcripts. They also, indicate to pay the wrong bills and that they are corrected eventually. In other words once I am stuck with the phones they own me and they can bill whatever they want. How can this company be gaining so many new customers with the BAIT and Switched strategy? These people need to be accountable.
Was with them 7 years left them because of the constant additional charges for 1 phone 1 line it's 210 month

I was told I was getting a free phone. Unfortunately, I was on automatic payment so I didn't see my bill. They not only charged me for the "free" phone, they charged me for insurance. We hate the phone and only got it because it was free. The store we got the "free" phone from closed and the company refused to refund the money. I am so disappointed in this company - I've been a customer for 9 years and im leaving after this scandalous transaction. I hate getting ripped off!

This is the Facebook page for the T-Mobile Lawsuit for those that have been overcharged or damaged by T-Mobile and forced to pay what you don't owe.

There will be a way to sign on to the class action in the coming weeks.  Start by joining and sharing your experience.

One of the line holders on my account passes away. TMobile says I have to continue to pay for the leased phone. This is how they treat customers of over 20 years. I recommend you don't add people to your account, because if they die, you'll be paying for it for 3 years.

My billing date is the 25th, so tomorrow they will issue me a FOURTH statement with a credit balance of $37.64. Is T-Mobile having money problems?

Another lady (supervisor) from Phillipean Kyla ID 6025158 told me, the order was under processed and must released 48hours. I would prefer to give them zero stars.
t-mobile ported my phone number without being able to activate my phone. They keep saying I need to come in to a store to activate so I keep reminding them I am out of the US. Now I have no access to my US phone number. I cannot even get out of t-mobile and back to my previous carrier because they cannot send me a text to confirm my number because I have no service. I have wasted literally hours on the phone and many dollars of international phone time trying to get them to fix this and they are incapable of even releasing my phone number to me. I've tried emailing or using their chat feature to avoid the expense of international calling, but I cannot login because my phone isn't working and they do not allow anyone to email or chat without first logging in. I am beyond angry with them. I responded to a request for feedback 3 weeks ago and checked their box requesting follow up contact from a manager, but have heard nothing. They are horrible.

Follow-up: Back in the US, still can't cancel because now they say my account is suspended and I somehow owe them almost $200. I still have not had service from them, never received any bills, and have spend more hours on the phone with customer service people who cannot help. Absolutely the worst experience I have had with a business.

I've been with the company for 23 years.  I was going to switch carriers, but was offered a new phone to stay while maintaining my current bill, which according to the agent "would have to be adjusted manually each month",but promised that it would be taken care of.  Subsequently each month it has not been taken care of and I have to call and ask for an adjustment each month.  A supervisor even went back and reviewed the phone call of the original agreement and credited my account and apologized and informed me that it would be adjusted,  but yet after 5 months I'm still dealing with it.  Today, I was told that it could no longer be adjusted and I'm stuck with a higher bill an extra phone line that I don't want or need.....and if I cancel it, I have to pay the full price of my new phone.  Deceptive and frustrating.

I've been a customer for 16 years. I recently switched companies. I canceled my service 3 days before the start of my billing cycle in July. In August, I noticed a charge to my bank account for a full month's service, which was canceled over a month prior. I was told that even though the number had been switched over for 3 days, the account was closed the day that the billing cycle started. How convenient! After spending over 1.5 hours talking to reps, telling me I need to go into a physical location (more inconvenience) for me, they acknowledged that if anything, I should have been billed at a pro-rated rate of just one day.  I disagreed and the supervisor rep finally told me they would credit my account the full payment amount, not even pro-rated. I called my bank and had them halt the payment of the charge because T-Mobile was able to take my money in a day but it would take up to 10 business days to return my money. 

Fast-forward to a few weeks later, and we get a bill in the mail for the full-amount, plus an additional $30 fee. We called T-Mobile again and had another supervisor waive the entire fee, which was supposed to have been waived a few weeks prior. This time we recorded the call at least.

Fast-forward to last night and we got yet another bill in the mail from T-Mobile. Called and they said we had a $0 balance and all was resolved. We chalked it up to the previous call being made after that last bill was sent to us. 

Today we received a letter from a collections agency from T-Mobile. So in a month and a half of the bill being "late" (even though they admitted 2 times being in the wrong), they sent us to collections. 

I've had perfect credit my entire life, and T-Mobile is trying to do what they can to change that over their mistake. This is how they treated a long-time loyal customer. Disgusting company.
they are not reliable  billed me for service i didn't have refuses to refund me. i'm on a fixed income and they keep giving me excuses.

Tried to switch services with T-Mobile, Service and signal from them on my phone was not good.. tried their service for 1 week They had one excuse after another not to give me a refund back of what they had charged me for, They somehow took a payment out of my account that prepay had been canceled from. They gave me a run around of probably two weeks different people then tried to charge me more than next month so they charged me $16.15 for a trial period of maybe 7 days Not a company company to deal with. all the way around they're not honest I spent 45 minutes on the phone with someone trying to straighten this out She acted like she knew what she was doing and was going to give me full credit back  Then by the time the conversation was ending her dollar amount changed!! Answer was, well we can only do this amount I do not recommend using their services. Too many different answers from too many people you have to go through  Please train your staff better, and be more knowledgeable before you put them out in a customer service field

I've been giving them the benefit of the doubt hoping things get better but it just keeps getting significantly worse... Customer support is great they do thier best but at the end of the day the service im paying for is absolutely terrible...
We will be transferring back to Verizon Wireless as soon as they have internet service in our area

I have a 795 credit score and it’s just not right I will go back to att where I can get anything I want and just pay taxes. I would have never switched to save 30 bucks a month.

It sucks and I have plenty of towers around me. It's not consistent. I lose signal and get buffering all the time watching tv. Many times it interferes with downloading. I will move gateway around the house constantly. Customer service is a joke. Was promised a local team but many times outsourced far away. The last thing they pulled is no longer allowing credit cards for auto pay. They had a data breach just last year (UPDATE) and another one just this week. I refuse to hand over the keys to my bank account to appease this mega rich corporation who cares less about my money. I am done with them.

If I could give 0 star or negative, I would.

Customer Service sucks. Signal sucks. Overpriced service.

I paid my phone in full but I could not get my phone unlocked. And it has been more than 7 days. I called customer care 5 times and they all are asking me to wait another 72 hours and put escalation ticket each time. But nothing has resolved.

Who gives Tmobile the right to hold my phone hostage for weeks while I have paid my phone in full?

Boycott the company!

We switched to Tmobile in Aug 2023 and wanted to love it.  Unfortunately, we felt misled at best and tricked at worst.  Now we are stuck for 2 years.   

1.  We were quoted a plan price (written down by sales salesperson).  We got a text saying sign up for automatic payments and you will get another $5 discount per line.  Come to find out, if we did not sign up for auto-payment it would cost more. No discount there. Bait and Switch.  
2. One line could not be activated the first day we purchased the other two phones.  Based on sales rep advice, we activated the line and they would roll the activated number to an actual new phone the following weekend.  We were charged for 2 activation fees. 
3.  The quoted price for the phones we traded in was inaccurate - to the tune of $400 less than quoted.  
4.  The customer service rep was very rude when we tried to understand what was going on and he basically said we should have known what the phone was worth and that when the phone was sent in, someone made the decision.  (We can understand if it was broken or water damaged of course the value should be reduced, but really if your employees do not know the value of a phone or mislead, or lie about the value, isn't that on the company??
5.  Coverage across rural SD and MN is not good.  Dropped calls several times over the miles. 
6.  So after month 2 bill, the cost is much higher than expected.  Oh yes and be sure your tech skills are strong as you will have to navigate their website and the app.
Charged me for a closed account and then said they cannot access the information to credit the $115.

Went to a T-Mobile store to upgrade our 4-line plan and phones. Had to wait 1 hour to talk with a salesperson. Went through the plan and phone description process and phone and upgrade process, which took 1.5 hours. 

When it came to paying the taxes and fees for the upgrades, it was only for 1 phone out of the 4 because they "can't accept an upgrade without the phones being physically present for inspection, so we can't process your request for all 4 phones." 

Since we're a family of 4 with 2 kids in college across the country, it's impossible to get all 4 phones in the store. "Well, you can do all of this online and self-assess your phone quality. Either way, you'll ship them into us for final assessment of your available credit." WTH? I can do everything online, but I can't in-store after almost 3 hours there??! Why don't they start with, as soon as you enter a store and tell them what you're there for, "Unfortunately, we can't help in you in store without all the phones present." It would have saved many hours for me and a lot of other people waiting while the salesperson was "helping me."

Also, you can't get a payment quote printed at T-Mobile, as you can at AT&T and Verizon. They scribble on Post-its to seemingly obfuscate the actual monthly costs. That practice is obviously designed to bait and switch. It's unscrupulous and fraudulent.

I'll be switching to AT&T or Verizon, where there's at least a paper trail to rely on and the customer service is at least a bit more professional. Perhaps I also won't be called, "my man" as in "I'm here to help you, my man. It'll be all good, my man." Why not just ask if I'd like to be called by my first name? It's not that hard.

T-Mobile is an absolutely terrible cell service provider. It is unfortunate really because the staff at the location I went to in West Bend, WI were actually very cordial and polite; however, the quality of cell service and the moral integrity of the company as a whole are absolutely miserable. I have never been charged the amount that was agreed upon for the last 10 months. Was promised a large refund in writing and only recently, after many months of waiting, was told I was not going to be receiving it. I do not mind paying good money for a good product but you do not even get that with T-Mobile. Terrible reception, constantly dropping calls even though "they have the largest cell tower network in the US". It is all lies. Horrible company that loops you in and then ties you down with "promotions" that have fine print and you do not get educated on at time of sale. I highly recommend US Cellular, Verizon or any other major competitor if you want your phone to actually work. Would give zero stars if I could. Please note I am writing this review after 10 months of patient professional understanding on my part and have had enough. Dropping calls with the VP of my company, being charged figures never discussed or agreed upon and the list goes on. The kind of company that is polite to your face or on the phone but are stabbing you in the back in the process. T-Mobile should be absolutely ashamed of themselves and the moment I have some time from my busy life I will be dropping them.

I went to make a payment on my account and they tell me I'm being charged $5.00 just for making a payment in the store. So, they took the $5.00 and then I didn't have enough to pay my bill. So, I  call them and they tell that I have to pay the full amount.  So my husband had to go all the way back and had to pay $4.00 to complete the payment.  He was charged another $5.00 again. So you are being charged inside the store and if you pay in the phone. . T mobile has really has gotten pretty bad. I have been with t mobile for 17 years. Other people will not be able to pay online in some cases they will be charged an extra $5.00 for paying their bill. It's an awful practice. And not everyone is doing that. That's what t mobile said.
They over billed us 4 months in a row despite us bringing to their attention multiple every time would issue credit for 3 months of over billing.

Cancelled my account for the same lack of customer support, crap service, dropped calls, etc. etc. Lost me thousands of dollars in customers who could not contact me. Moved to Visible. Then they charged me over $50 for cancelling my account without providing a service. I explained to the representative that if I receive further communication of any kind from T-mobile it will be considered spam and I will take whatever action I can against this pathetic company.

I went to the Mesa, AZ store on Sunday, May 14, 2023 (Mothers' Day) and was told of a Mothers' Day promotion - free, iPhone upgrade with trade-in plus a free Apple Watch for only $10 more per month. Great deal, right? People: do not leave the store without written paperwork that outlines what you're getting. I was previously on the 55+ plan at $50/month, so I was getting a good deal for $60/month or so I thought. I was being billed $106 every month! After calls to the 611 number - they couldn't help and suggested I go back to the store and speak to a manager. Went back in June and with an apology Colton gave me a credit and said that the discounts should kick-in in the next month. Well, here we are in September, I went back to the Mesa store where a new manager, Juwann is telling me there was NEVER a Mothers' Day promotion and asked me if I had a receipt that outlines the promotion I was getting. Okay, I guess that's my bad for trusting TMobile.

Somehow, my T-Mobile account switched the primary account holder from myself to my son so that I can no longer access my account - my login is now invalid and when I try to reset the login,they send the security code to his phone so I never see it. I called their 'customer service' to get this fixed and they couldn't do anything over the phone and told me I'd need to physically go into their store to get it reset....when I tried that, they STILL couldn't do anything and told me I'd need to bring my son in to verify the account. Screw that, I'm closing my account and switched to Consumer Cellular and the difference was astounding! I'm saving more than half of what T-Mobile was charging, getting unlimited everything and talked with a REAL service rep that actually spoke English and could be understood. I have absolutely nothing good or remotely positive to say about T-Mobile and wish I'd dumped their lousy rear ends years ago....T-Mobile's customer service is useless and absolutely awful and incompetent...a truly horrible customer experience.
Poor customer service, not being able to get 20$ off with auto pay from credit card. Incompetence with canceling service. Overpriced!!! Today is the last day I will deal with them!

Recent 2 line switch xfer at t-mobile from Verizon to t-mobile mid level 55+ plan ( supposed to be $100 total) has been a disaster (performed in store).   

1. First bill showed up on app and showed $169 due end of month (on autopay).  Called t-mobile "mother-ship" and was told it would be fixed and that it would be $100, but App and website login still showing $169.

2. Doesn't appear what i was told store "up to $800 credit to pay off of the newer phones I own which is about $650 on" is ever happening (bait and switch?)

3. Service at my home the same as verizon (one bar, no 5G) but not according to their coverage area map.  Phone is unusable in my neighborhood unless on my home wifi or someone else's wifi.

All in all a very long, stressful, and possibly costly event in my life with no added value unless  T-mobile gets my billing fixed. And I win the "fight" for phone credit.

The worst carrier on the planet I was a T-Mobile customer a while ago and I'd say about maybe 4 years ago this was before they bought out Sprint and they were an okay company until they bought out Sprint then they became money hungry and raising my bill every month their price lock guarantee does not exist to customers I wait for 2 hours on hold to talk to somebody on top of that they won't upgrade any phones or try to even give you a new promotion for new lines I should have never made the switch I'm regretting it now do not switch stay with the current provider you're with cuz they can probably give you a better deal than these blow holes

There is no customer service. Had two phones with them 4 yrs finally canceled. Got tired of the dead zones and no phone connection service. Tried the 5g network cause they offered $50. rebate. In 6 mos haven't received rebate. Made several calls to them with no success. In short they stink and don't care to improve.

made a call to ask questions about the home internet router to possibly buy and the guy didn't seem to have the answers so he hung up on me mid call, called again and got a different guy and said i also had a complaint and he intentionally hung up on me too LOL. good thing im still with verizon for everything.
Took 1 hour to get a Sim Card for my son. System was very very slow. Terrible

I purchased a plan with T-Mobile about 10 years ago and obviously didn’t learn my lesson. There was no service in the area and T-Mobile was well aware of the problem. They continued to defraud and take advantage of the consumer, threatening to ruin your credit if you didn’t pay for useless services. Fast forward to 2.5 years ago when I decided to take another chance with T-Mobile. I purchased a 3 year agreement which included a premium plan, bogo phones and a $10 discount for auto pay. T-Mobile decided 2.5 years into the agreement that they wouldn’t honor auto pay unless they had access to your bank account. Please don’t give them access to your bank accounts to save $10. Ever wonder why you couldn’t pick up your iPhone at Apple? Chances are they sold you a refurbished phone and passed it off as new. If you decide to pay off your phone they threaten to not give you credit for the payments you already made. If you decide to cancel auto pay since you’re not getting credit unless you provide your bank account, they threaten to charge you fees for paying in store. Please read the lawsuit between NYC and T-Mobile regarding the numerous complaints of deceptive and fraudulent business practices before committing to T-Mobile because I have experienced it all.

It is a shame that after many many years of being a T-Mobile customer that we were forced to make a change.  Their service, which had been really good just kept getting worse and worse, to the point of having multi day total blackouts of all services.  Had no choice but to switch.  And what used to be outstanding customer service, has become the worst I have experienced in a long while.  We have been trying to cancel all lines and the entire account for 3 months now.  They have been playing games, keeping a couple lines active and charging a very high price for something that should have been canceled.  (I am sure they are doing this so they don't have to count us as a lost customer.)  When I call and demand the account to be closed, they refuse, or they hang up.  I am shocked at just how bad this company has become.

Daughter had T-Mobile iPhone and was trying to just get the phone unlocked but T-Mobile gave us the run around for over a week constantly doing the same process over again with no results. The customer service is crap and the reps have no clue on what they’re doing

I was with Sprint for 20 years never did I lose service at my home since T-Mobile is taking it over I lose service all the time T-Mobile is sucks it is the worst service you could freaking Buy unfortunately they're the only one that works real well out where I'm at from what I understand I have been thinking about changing to Verizon I hear they're really well in my area now I do not understand why T-Mobile is not using the towers it's Sprint used at which isn't far from my home it is sad when I'm driving down my road and I lose mobile service right in the city I am sick and tired of it if I would have had an emergency I could not call for help because T-Mobile sucks they do not cover half of what they say they do they lie

The T-Mobile Sales and Activation team voluntarily provides misinformation to influence customers' decisions to take up service lines.

Even though the activation call recordings confirm the offered promotion, the T-Mobile Service team cannot provide it due to technical issues. This has resulted in paying higher bills than agreed upon.

Furthermore, T-Mobile's sales team does not provide written communication regarding the offer. Instead, they ask customers to wait for a few months for the billing cycle to reflect the promotion, which will close the cancellation window and return period. This has become a winning strategy for T-Mobile.

Additional facts:
The entertainment perks often catch the customer's attention, and the auto-pay discount program may lead them to overlook what is being billed.

Was looking to upgrade my phone in March. Available plans at the time claimed magenta Max premium plan would provide a discount for upgrading phone. 
Once the data plan was switched, however, they changed the benefits so I would have to upgrade AGAIN to an even more expensive go5 plan. Now I'm paying $20 more every month for absolutely no benefit and can't upgrade.

I am leaving Verizon for terrible customer service and wished I had read these reviews before committing to T-Mobile.  I haven't received my new phones yet and already I have had nothing but hassle.  
First off the email confirming my order was incorrect.  I ordered two lines and Salvador the customer service rep helping me ordered three.
When I called to get this corrected I couldn't get help because my pin didn't work.
I wrote down the pin number I created but on their end they said it was incorrect.
I ordered online to avoid going nearly 40 miles to the closest store.  Turns out the only way to correct their error was to go to the T Mobile store. 
I was told I could reset my pin at the store by the customer service rep.  When I called the store to make an appointment I was told they couldn't reset pins.
So for the very reason I left Verizon is happening with T Mobile.
Out of the fry pan and into the fire is where I am.

I joined T-mobil and needed some help porting a number. Justine and Nicole went out of their way to help me solve my problem. They are at 466 Western Blvd Jacksonville N.C. 28546. Please go see these awesome ladies. They will help you get the best service you can get. Thank You Justine and Nicole. J.Davis

I learned the first time my son ported his service to Verizon that you actually have to call T-Mobile (then Sprint) to actually CANCEL the billing. It doesn't cancel just by porting the phone number to another carrier. So when my daughter moved to her husband's ATT plan, I quickly CALLED and cancelled her line (about a year ago). I don't actually review my bill because it is on auto pay. I happen to look at it last month only to find I was still being charged for her phone service. I immediately called and they told me that NO ONE had access to my billing because I was being transferred to T-Mobile billing and I would be notified via text when that transfer was complete. I never received a text. I decided to check today and low and behold! I am on the T-Mobile billing platform and my daughter's line has been removed. They told me that her line was only "successfully ported" last month. Not true- I called a year ago to complete the port and get it off my bill since I got burned before. The cust service rep tried to tell me that when you port your service it automatically cancels the service and there was no way she could have been on ATT. I have the ATT bill from Dec 2022 that her number is on. This is absolute robbery to continue billing for service after someone has changed carriers. I would give a 0-star rating if I could.

This my be the worst T-Mobile experience I have ever had and will never have again. 

What a scam this company is. I went into a local branch and worked with the manager who detailed in writing a complete package price which included moving to T-Mobile with 5 new lines and 5 new phones. 

I scheduled an appointment to return to ensure that the process went smoothly. When I did, we quickly discovered that the pricing I had been promised just a couple of days earlier was no longer valid. To determine that, the worker (one of the Manny's) told me that the only way to get me a complete price was to both check my credit and scan 5 new SIM cards with the promise that the credit check would not be reported and they would not charge me for the cards.

After discovering that my monthly bill would be nearly double what I was promised we declined the service and walked away thinking that would be the end of it.

Almost 4 months later I am still getting a bill and past due notices from T-Mobile for the account (that I do not have) and for the SIM cards (which I also do not have.)

I have spent nearly 4 hours on the phone and online with T-Mobile customer service and still cannot get the charges cancelled.

I also just discovered that they did in fact run my credit and so now this is reporting as a late payment.

I cancelled my T-Mobile account.  After paying all charges over the phone that were due to close my account, and after I had already ported my telephone number over to a nother provider, T-Mobile charged my bank account without authorization.  I tried resolving this via chat and phone and they refused to provide a refund. They are requiring that I visit a store in person just to undo a charge they weren't supposed to make in the first place.  My understanding is that any unauthorized charge by a business to a consumer account is fraud, and it's upsetting that I have to waste time out of my workday to undo a fraudulent charge.

I have T-Mobile home Internet service and I set up payment arrangements for a bill and was charged a $7 late fee for using what was supposed to be a free service on my next bill. This was not something said when the payment arrangements were set up. I googled and plain as day it said free but  noticed there has been a constant complaint among customers regarding this fee. I spoke to a great customer service agent who removed it and stressed it would remain if it should happen again. This really does not set well with me either. I am contemplating getting rid of their service and giving Cox another try. I recently referred someone who got their service and didn't even get a single referral fee or anything. I will not be referring anyone else based off this treatment alone. I personally don't have time to deal with companies who treat their customers like this.

Tried Home Internet.  Horrible signal, cancelled service within 1 week.  For several months after that they sent me a statement showing a credit balance of $44.. I guess because it's $50 per month and I had it for a few days.  A couple of months ago they sent me a debit card worth $44.  Yesterday they sent me a bill for $44 plus a $7 late fee.  When I inquired, after 1 hour they told me I never paid for the service to start with and it was their error that caused them to send me the debit card.  (I don't know whether I paid for this back last December or not).  If a merchant sends you something you didn't order don't you get to keep it?  Is it different for T-Mobile.  I have periodically had their cell service, but I won't ever have them again.  I paid the $44 and filed a complaint with the public service commission.

Never go to The T-Mobile store at Gessner & Highway 249 Houston Texas, except you have plenty time to waste. The store number is 7914. They are deliberately slow and the manager Rod Thomas sits around doing nothing instead of help out the other slow staff, when you ask him to help out he'd quietly ask you to sit down and wait or go elsewhere. They have cameras in the shop so you can verify what im saying, its no shades. They practically take 3 hours plus to attend to one customer.  We waited for ever just to buy a Sim card. I've been to the sane store 3 times and it's the same treatment.

WARNING I changed service in Oct 2022 from Verizon to T Mobile when I got the offer on the phone from T Mobile they said they would pay off my phone with Verizon if I switched so I switched. A month prior my home was destroyed by Hurricane Ian, T mobile never paid Verizon for the phone and still I get calls every day from a collection agency. T Mobile is pure 100 percent fraud and should be investigated for this kind of fraud. I wanted to report this near a year ago but had to much going on.
Wrongful billing.  Service that was good is no longer good.  I left and recommend choosing a different provider.

Ellensburg WA Aug 30, 2023. Older person: later in the afternoon inquiring about home internet. This light brown haired / blonde chunky woman kept saying “unfortunately” When asked not to say that, she repeated it. Her attitude ‘I’m just doing my job and if you don’t like it you can leave.” 
She was very satisfied to pick a fight with someone not up to her shiny faced appearances standards.  So the customer left, never to return. She successfully cleansed the place of “those people.”

So simply put don't go to t mobile I have several lines and 2 internet services on a business account I have 1 phone number that is messed up Julio from CEO office says it's coverage how dumb does Julio think I am 8 lines of service 1 line has coverage issue

I called 611 (4 times in a row) while living in Puerto Rico, but only speak English.  I called to upgrade my plan.  Press 2 for English. Only Spanish-speaking reps answered.  When I asked to please speak with an English speaking representative they either A) just put me on hold until eternity and then hung up.  or B) they just hung up.  This is all while I pressed #2 for English.   Needless to say, I did not get my phone service upgraded.

I subscribed t-mobile go5g plus plan during my 20 days trip to europe. I spent total 138 minutes on call, the rate is 0.25 per minutes, total is 34.50, but I ended up paying 239.84. Constantly receiving their bill, even after I cancelled the service. Spent hours on call with t-mobile support, nothing pleasant.  I am not used to write review, but this time I feel compelled to.

edits.text: It was a nightmare for me. The sales rep was a scammer.  I planned for international traveling and checked with tmobile through online chatting, I explained to him that I need international roaming for about 2 weeks, but he pushed a plan for 4 lines to me. After I received the email I called in immediately requesting cancellation of the service but I still received 4 SIM cards the other day. I thought naively that if i don't use their SIM card, they shouldn't charge me, but NO. They started charging my from the day the salesrep set up my account, 200 a month no matter you use it or not.  I called in 3 times and spent hours asking for correction but it seems there is no way any of the representative are able or willing to correct it. Stay away from this company, it is a SCAMMER!!

text: When I tell you the most horrible experience I have ever had there customer service is horrible they got all the damn foreign people taking calls and they know half of wat your talking about they are rude as hell and I wouldn't recommend them to anyone. Not to mention they applied my payment to someone elses account and never did the proper things to get my money applied Im having to request a refund on my own

If I could give them 0 stars I would.  I was a sprint customer and loved it.  Every since they merged with t-mobile it's been nothing but problems. They lie about promotions and no one knows what they are talking about. I call 4 times for the same problem and get 4 different answers. I was with sprint for 17 years and now that they're T-Mobile it is time to find a new carrier.

I just switched over from ATT. I was promised a reimbursement for paying off my iPhone as well as a $200 gift card. Now that I’m a customer, the phone calls are VERY different. I’ve been jumping through hoops for three days to get the reimbursement which finally they just told me it was denied (for dumb reasons of course). I ended up crying on the phone to them, I was so upset at myself for believing them in the first place. The guy on the phone then also told me I would not get the $200 because you can’t combine them. I told him I know it’s not his fault but the people that sell to new customers say something totally different. Anywho, after much crying and refusing to accept that I would be out $300 of my own money, this guy finally put the reimbursement in manually ! So that’s what it takes guys. Just so ya know. Oh and no $200 gift card as promised. Oh and that Wi-Fi hook up I got from them, ya that doesn’t work either. It’s one thing to tell people they will get $200 free money and then take it back, but it’s a whole other evil corporate move to trick people into paying off their (very expensive) iPhones with reimbursement promises that are then taken back. Screw you to the people sitting in their chairs coming up with this trickery.

Had these jockers for over 2 years.
Hardly anything  good to say about  them.
The most unreliable coverage,  their customer service reps are utterly clueless. 
They will sign you up to plans T mobile doesn't  have.
When they throttle you back ( aka prioritization) they will literally  cut you off from data/ internet. 
Their " unlimited plans" are just a lie , T mobile doesn't  have " unlimited data plans" - none!
Their app is as useless as they are , you can't  connect  to get your account  information. 
" service unavailable " is more than a common error message  you'll get trying...
Their reps ( overseas of course) care only about the reviews they are asking you to write at the end of every single phone conversation with them.
In short , just a bunch of  lying scammers  disguised as a reputable business.

Worst higher team support, my phone was locked when it was supposed to be unlocked when I had paid it off 1 yr. ago, When I had switched over to another service, my phone was still locked. Now they said it’s going to take 5 days plus.  I m out of luck without a phone. Be careful and read the other reviews to make your decision/

T-Mobile for them to be one of the largest cell phone company everytime I turn around I'm getting a text for my phone that my bill is higher than what I was told it would be. Then when I call in I get feed some bull. I beginning to think that they are full of it and I need to change companies don't believe nothing they say. Lies that's all they do

One year ago my wife and I switched from ATT telephone carrier to T-Mobile and from Windstream Kinetic Internet Service to T-Mobile Internet.  We regret both changes.  For early September 2022, T-Mobile internet was great averaging 100 MPS; by late September it was half that or less, and by mid-October, it was averaging the same as our recent Windstream Kinetic, 6 MPS with increasingly more and more times when we had no internet.  We returned the T-Mobile router and discontinued their  internet in early November and signed up with Spectrum which our neighbors liked  a lot, and now so do we.
Telephone service has been adequate until recently, with occasional dead spots like the parking lot of the St. Lukes Hospital Infusion Center where I go weekly.   This past month telephone service has gotten much worse with frequent messages "Not Registered With Service" when I try to call from home and other locations.
WHY?  Last October when I spoke to a T-Mobile technician about my declining internet speeds, he confirmed that the service was oversubscribed.  I suppose that is what is happening to their telephone service now. They just opened a new T-Mobile store in our small town, just a few miles away from several other such stores.  We have two new phones as of a year ago which won't be paid off for another year so we can't change carriers without paying heir very high retail price for those phones. 
Stay away from T Mobile until they get their together!

Been a loyal customer with T-Mobile for a few years and now having a big issue. They want me to make another payment of $170 that I have already made. Why am I going to make a double payment and threatened to suspend my service when this issue is on their part. They need to do the right thing and remove that $170 and put my account back in good standing. No wonder T-Mobile is being sued.

edits.text: I understand when a customer makes a payment arrangement and it doesn't go through due to unforseen circumstances yet they can't go in and redo the arrangement or give you more time,TMobile just shuts customer off such bad customer service they won't even note your account that you are working with your church to get help with a payment so your service doesn't get shut off during this time. TMobile get it together and do better in helping your customers, a lot of US are still struggling from the pandemic and now a recession.

text: I would give zero ıf ı can. I have both mobile and house ınternet from them. My house internet has gone and they did not do anyrhıng since one week!!!

Service is fine, not the reason I'm giving a bad review, their custom service/support is extremely bad and lacking. I've called them so many times to tell them that I cannot pay at certain times of the month and they continue to schedule bills at these times, forcing me to pay late fees and go without service a lot of the time. This has been an ongoing issue for 7 (or more) months now, and I am getting extremely fed up.

Hello:
I have been a T-Mobile customer for 10 years; I have 6 business lines with them.
When I signed up with them, they were wonderful; the service was great and reliable, but during the past 3 years, their service and reliability have been diminishing.
In so many occasions, the phone calls does not go though even though we are testing our own phones; the phones do not ring and the callers are directed to voice mail; voice mails are not notified for as long as 3 days.
Today I had a very important call which I took the day off just so to be able to receive the call.  12 minutes past the due time, I received an email that they had called me and it had directed them to voice mail.  I was looking at my phone the whole time.  This was the straw that broke the cannel's back, and now I am searching for substitute to my business elsewhere.

Sincerely,
Michael

Absolute garbage, save your money, you'll never get decent service or customer care. Had Sprint, GARBAGE, but T-Mobile beat them. Had Sprint, end of contract, requested to be transferred to T-Mobile. Assured I was now with T-Mobile, NOOOOO, still contracted with Sprint and had to do 2 more years with their trash services. I vowed I would change companies when the contract was up. Contract was up in April 2023, waited to August to change providers do to a move, damage to my house in a store, etc. Enduring crappy service, dropped calls, people thinking I had hung up on them when it was the phone. Phone loosing connection, on and on and on. I have rather chopped off a finger then have to call their customer service to get help because it was going to take DAYS to get it fixed.
Besides crappy cell service... They don't pro-rate when you switch providers. They will charge you the full bill if you used 1 day or 29 days into the new billing cycle. Of course, you are just supposed to know that. When I called to cancel service, they wanted to hear nothing but when I would be paying for this month's service at the full rate to close the account. Of course, the rep felt that I should have just known that they don't pro-rate. "It's an industry standard" he says... It took me a week to just get into my Sprint, now T-Mobile account to get the information I need to switch providers. The rep from T-Mobile promised to call me back on a Thursday, or in two days and I would be able to get into my account…but low and behold he got back to me the next week. So had to wait an extra week to activate my new phones through another provider. $$$ They want your money...but they don't want to have to provide anything for it. If I was not a person who paid my bill, I could understand this behavior, but I pay two weeks in advance every month.... I now understand why my boss dumped T-Mobile for his company... DON'T WASTE YOUR MONEY ON THIS GARBAGE CELL SERVICE!!

I am a disabled verteran.  I've been with TMobile since 2015. I added my family to my account including my spouse and 2 sons.  Everything was fine and then our oldest son graduated high school in 2018 and joined the Air Force in late 2019.  Sometime in 2020 or 2021, my son called us wanting to be taken off my account so that he could establish his own account.  So we requested that he be taken off of the account.  At the time, he was stationed in Los Angeles while we were here in Texas.  Everything was done over the phone.  TMobile understood what we wanted & removed my son from our account and created a new account for him.  The problem is that when they created the new account, instead of using his social security number, they used mine.  Keep in mind, me and my son have the same name so I believe the TMobile representative thought my son was me (At least that is what I would like to believe as anything else would be nefarious).  So this whole time my sons account was setup under my SSN and I had no knowledge of it nor could I manage it or receive updates regarding it.  If I would have gotten any type of update on this acct, we wouldn't be here.  Fast forward to now, my son overextended himself financially and the account was cancelled.  Now all of a sudden I'm getting calls from a collections agency.  But my account is in good standing.  Once I found out about this other account, I reached out to TMobile for resolution and they are telling me that since the account was valid and had usage and in my name, there is nothing they can do.  The customer service people are pleasant but all are telling me that they can't help and neither can their managers.  WTH!  I am so angry right now.  They want me to write to their legal department and wait for reply.  So I'm writing this first so every potential/existing customer will know what can happen to them at TMobile.  You need to make this right! This was your mistake, fix it.  I've been a loyal customer but am now considering leaving.

They simply refuse to send me a final bill. They got paid via autopay, but they won't send me my final bill. Said they can't email bills-huh? Beyond frustrated. Their so called customer service is non existent and clearly have very little training. The worst part is I wasted so much time calling in 5 times and each time I asked to speak with a supervisor and they REFUSED to transfer the call. Said the supervisor doesn't have anymore information or access to any more info than they were. What???

Awful service and high rates
I live in a big city and often get only 1 bar of service. At times the phone indicates I have two or three bars, but nothing loads. Their customer service is awful and they charge you extra if you pay your bill with a credit card.

I have been with T-Mobile for yrs, never had a complaint till now. Back at the end of July my husband and I went to get new phones. My husband got the new Samsung and I pre ordered the fold (which didn't drop till 8/11) we also got all the accessories for both. On 8/1 my husband's phone and all the accessories came in. On 8/11 I went into my tmobile app to see when they were going to send my fold. It said completed 8/1. So I called tmobile, the person I talked to was confused also. It showed phone delivered, but had no esm number or any identifying information on the phone. I was told a ticket would go in and I would be reached put to in the next 5 to 7 days. On the 8th day I reached out again. Spent an hour on hold while they were trying to figure stuff out. Finally got on the phone with a supervisor who told me to he honest no one had even looked into my ticket. Mind you while they are investigating they are holding my 750 deposit and I can even go get a new one. Here I am still waiting for someone to contact me on this issue and still nothing. Beyond upset with how this whole situation is being handled.

Do not do any business with Sprint or T-Mobile. Sprint robbed me of over 500.00 dollars. When attempting to get a refund I was lied to several times. Calling customer service is the most painful task anyone can do in a day. Every time you call, you have to start your story all over again and the wait times can be astronomical. Are they not taking any notes about the 30 times I have had to call call? And dozens of attempts later, still not resolved. With a company this large, you think they would be obligated to refund their customers with money that was wrongfully charged/billed. Months of calling and getting transferred from one customer service rep to another, I have been left with nothing but wasted time and a bank account that is short over 500.00. They have even admitted their mistake in overcharging me but still no resolution. Please don't waste your time and money. If there are any issues you will be promised everything and anything, but you most likely will be left with frustration and regrets. I have always been friendly and kind and always paid my bills on time for years. But to them that doesn't matter. They would rather lose a customer than resolve a wrong doing. To this company, YOU DON'T MATTER. What a shame. I am looking for assistance on how to file a formal complaint but of course SPRINT and TMOBILE are not assisting with that either. Even after acknowledging that they made a mistake in overcharging me for hundreds of dollars.

If they try and tell you that they have a 20 or 14 day trial. Don't do it ! They screwed me big time! Saying it takes two weeks to cancel the phone. So why advertise 14 days? So t mobile can take their bill and shove it. Signal was lost in stores . Every store I walked into I had nothing. I was caught in a tornado because their service didn't work. I cancelled my phone within 7 days and they said I have to pay for the service used. Even though it was a trial

Low Network, Low Battery, Low Everything ruins my lively-hood experience.😭

When I called T-Mobile earlier this year in search of a new service provider, they flat out lied about the coverage availability for our area. When we opted to go with them, we noticed issues immediately. I called them after only 2 weeks to advise that we could not get service and would have to cancel. The customer service rep apologized profusely over the sales pitch we received because T-Mobile does not have coverage for our area. I inquired about a pro-rated refund because we paid the entire month and was told that while it would take about 6 weeks, the refund for the unused portion would be processed. After waiting 2 months I called back several times and was advised that the refund was in process. Finally after 4 months I called back and was advised that we were not entitled to a refund but they could not explain why. My multiple requests to speak with a supervisor were denied and 2 calls disconnected. T-Mobile will tell you whatever they need to to earn your business and fail to do the right thing when they cannot fulfil their obligations to its customers.

We were greeted by a lovely sales rep yesterday to help us purchase our new telephones She was very informative and helpful in transferring the information from our old phones to the new ones which took a very long time because our previous co was not very helpful.
She was very kind considerate and very knowledgeable. Also, very patient T Mobile of Southern Pines NC is very lucky to have her as an emplyee

Dealing with Tech Support is like speaking to a wall!!
They don't know how to fix issues!
Make sure you pay them though!!

Absolutely the worst service ever!  I ordered internet service, and then realized I didn't need it.  I have been on the phone with T-mobile trying to stop service and return the modem, with no luck.  I have called at least 4 time, on hold up to 40 min, talking with customer service overseas where I can't understand them and the room they are working in has 100 people, so you can't hear them either.  I was just on the phone for 4th time (35 min) and hopefully now will get my label to return.  That was supposed to happen yesterday, so we will see what happens.  Don't use T-mobile.
